title: ITR Filing Guide — Which ITR Form to Use (FY 2025-26)

ITR-2.for: Individuals and HUFs NOT having business/professional income

ITR-3.for: Individuals and HUFs having business/professional income
ITR-3.note: Must if you have intraday trading or F&O income

ITR-4_Sugam.for: Individuals, HUFs, and firms (other than LLP) opting for presumptive taxation
schemes: Section 44AD: Business income (turnover up to ₹3 crore if 95%+ digital receipts, else ₹2 crore). Deemed profit: 8% (cash) / 6% (digital)
Section 44ADA: Professionals (gross receipts up to ₹75 lakh if 95%+ digital). Deemed profit: 50%

ITR-5.for: Partnership firms, LLPs, AOPs, BOIs, artificial juridical persons

ITR-6.for: Companies other than those claiming Section 11 exemption (charitable trusts)

ITR-7.for: Trusts, political parties, institutions, charitable organizations, universities, research associations

key_due_dates_FY2025_26.penalty_234F: ₹5,000 if filed after due date (₹1,000 if income ≤ ₹5L)

mandatory_disclosures.bank_accounts: All bank accounts (including those closed during the year)
mandatory_disclosures.foreign_assets: Schedule FA (Foreign Assets) — must report all foreign bank accounts, properties, shares
mandatory_disclosures.capital_gains: Script-wise details of all equity/MF transactions
mandatory_disclosures.cash_deposits: Disclose cash deposits exceeding ₹10L in savings accounts

common_mistakes: Not reporting exempt income (LTCG up to ₹1.25L, agricultural income)
Missing bank interest from savings accounts
Forgetting to verify ITR within 30 days of filing
Not reporting capital gains from mutual fund SWP/redemptions
Claiming deductions not available in New Regime
